highlights.text: I wrote on Monday at 4pm that: “ Bear case tomorrow: Short-term, begins below 6453” and “If 6453 fails, we start down.” Yesterday morning, ES lost 6453 and instantly began a sell down to 6420 to set the low of day.
As readers know very well though, every “elevator down” sell in ES (which is what we saw this morning) has a sibling: The short squeeze.
When ES sells, it violently cuts through every level for a few minutes to a few hours, then it puts in a vertical squeeze on a Failed Breakdown.

In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll discuss a series of setups we’ve had since Friday that span all my setup types: We had a Failed Breakdown Friday, A Level Reclaim Long Sunday (its key to know the difference between this and a Failed Breakdown), then a Breakdown Short yesterday.

post_title: The Dip Was Bought in SPX. Dead Cat, or Bottom? August 21st Plan
